purpose: Golden labels for Bug J — named-product follow-up intent classification. Each case is a user utterance after a top3 has been recommended; the classifier must resolve to the correct target_product_id.

cases.user_message: Tell me more about HealthFirst Individual
cases.notes: Exact full-name match. Should resolve at confidence ~1.0.

cases.user_message: I want to know about HealthFirst
cases.notes: First-word substring. Distinguishable since no other top3 product starts with 'Health'.
All lowercase, no punctuation. Must match HeartShield CI Plan.
One-letter typo (helthfirst vs HealthFirst). Edit-distance fallback expected. Confidence likely 0.7-0.85.
Spaces inserted in name (cancer care vs CancerCare). Tokenization-tolerant match.
Ordinal — should resolve to top3[0]. Classifier must NOT treat 'first' as a name fragment.

cases.user_message: tell me about the second option

cases.notes: All three top3 candidates are term_life. Single-word 'Term' matches NONE uniquely. Force-clarify band — confidence < 0.5 expected, classifier should ask for clarification rather than picking one.
First-word match against RetireSmart Pension. Other PENS products don't share this prefix.
Compound first-word match. ChildStar Future Plan vs other CHLD products.
Generic concept question, NOT a named-product reference. D4 lock: POLICY_QUESTION routes to free-form sub-agent, not B5.

cases.user_message: yeah uh tell me about that LifeGuard one
cases.notes: Disfluencies (yeah/uh/that/one). LifeGuard Plus Term should still resolve.
Two-word substring. MoneyBack Classic uniquely matches; other ENDT products don't contain 'money back'.

cases.user_message: no I asked for ULIP not term
cases.notes: Pushback / wrong-product-type signal. Top3 doesn't contain ULIP — classifier should flag for clarification or trigger re-ranking, NOT pick a TERM product.
